Assistant Dashboard
📥 Downloads & Print
Everything to print before the party — wristbands, codes, recipes, guides and certificates.
Documents open in a new tab and the print dialog appears automatically. Choose Save as PDF from the printer list to save the file. Certificates can also be saved as JPG photos.

Names & codes — cut & tape onto kids' wrists.

Certificates
One per child. Open to print, or save directly as PDF or JPG.
📱 Phone/tablet: Save as JPG downloads to your camera roll. PDF opens for printing via browser share menu.
🔐 Owner Setup — JustMadness.com
🎬 Content Setup
Upload videos and dog character photos. This screen is for the website owner only — not parents or kids.

🎬 Mission Videos (12)

Upload or link the 12 mission videos. These play during the kids' mission automatically.

🐾 Dog Character Photos (up to 16)

Upload one photo per dog character. Each child is automatically matched to one dog in order. These appear on the identity reveal screen at the end of the mission.

Assistant Dashboard
👦 Children & Teams
Enter children's names and choose how many teams.
🐾🐾🐾🐾🐾🐾🐾🐾🐾🐾🐾🐾
🐾 Welcome to Dog's Bakery!
This is your Assistant Dashboard — the secret control room that only you can see! Here's how it works:
1️⃣
Set up the party — work through all 9 prep screens at your own pace. Add children's names, choose game levels, set cake recipes, hide the decorations and set your Hangdog riddle.
2️⃣
Run a dry run — before the party, you can go through the entire mission yourself to check everything looks right. Navigate using the arrows at the bottom of the screen.
3️⃣
Lock & hand over — when you're ready, go to Prep 9/9 and press Lock Party. The setup screens disappear and the mission begins! Kids take over from here.
🔓
Always unlockable — need to make a change? Tap the 🔓 button in the top bar at any time — even during the party — to come back here and edit anything. The kids won't see a thing.
💾 Everything saves automatically — you can close this app and come back later. Your setup will be exactly where you left it.
🔒 Your data stays on this device
All names, photos and setup information are stored only on this device — in your browser's private storage. Nothing is uploaded to any server, cloud or the internet. No account is needed. No data leaves your device.

📸 Photos — children's photos you upload stay on this device only and are never shared or transmitted anywhere.

🗑️ To delete everything — use the Reset button on Prep 9/9, or clear your browser's site data in settings. Once cleared, all names and photos are permanently removed.

Children's Names

How Many Teams?

Assistant Dashboard
👦👧 Gender & Photos
Set each child's gender so the right dog is assigned. Photos are completely optional — the mission works perfectly without them.
👉 Generated your codes in Screen 1 first? Great — now set each child's gender and upload their photo here.
👦👧 Tap Boy or Girl for each child — this assigns the right dog name and dog photo.

📸 Optional: Tap the circle to upload a photo — shown on the welcome scan screen. If skipped, a 🐾 paw shows instead. The mission is not affected either way.

🤫 Keep it secret! Do NOT tell children their dog names — it's the big reveal in Mission 7!
Assistant Dashboard
🔑 Game Codes
These are the secret codes agents must find and enter during the mission to unlock baking ingredients.
🔐 How codes work:
Each game has its own set of real codes hidden somewhere in the room — agents must find them to unlock the next ingredient.
Game 3 also has dummy codes to trick the agents — entering a dummy triggers a funny dog error!

📋 What you need to do:
1. Download the code sheet below and print it (or keep it on a second device).
2. Check the next screen (Setup 3/8) for full game instructions — it tells you exactly where and how to hide each code for every game and difficulty level.
3. Hide the codes before the party starts — the agents must not see them!
👥 One code per team: The number of codes generated for each game matches the number of teams you set in Prep 1/9. If you change the number of teams later, come back here and regenerate the codes.
Assistant Dashboard
🎮 Games & Level Setup
Choose a difficulty level for each game. Instructions and equipment list update automatically.
💡 Level guide: 🟢 Mild levels are less messy and ideal for indoor activities. 🤪 Mad levels add a fun twist with a bit more chaos. 🤯 Bonkers levels involve water, flour or food mess — better suited for outdoor or easy-clean surfaces!
🎂💥

Bonus: CAKE FIGHT! 🍰

Want to end the party with an epic (and very silly) cream fight? Enable this to unlock a safety rules screen at the very end of the mission — so everyone knows the rules before the chaos starts!
You will need: sponges + spray cream cans (1 can per 2–3 children).

Assistant Dashboard
🎂 Select Cake Recipes
Set up the cake recipes that teams will choose from during the mission. You can edit, remove, or add your own!
🎂 How cake selection works:
During the mission, each team picks which cake they want to bake — giving kids the excitement of making their own choice! The recipes reveal line by line as they play, so it feels like a real baking challenge.

💡 Good to know: The cakes are very similar in ingredients — just small tweaks between them — so no team gets an unfair advantage. It's all about the fun of choosing! And if two teams pick the same cake? Totally fine — a bit of friendly competition makes it even better. 🐾
Assistant Dashboard
📸 Apron Photos & Hats
Upload a photo of each child wearing their decorated apron and hat.
Assistant Dashboard
📸 Baking Session Photos
These photos appear during the Baking Montage. Upload them live during the mission!
📸 No action needed here!


💡 Tip: Make sure your phone is on the same WiFi network as this device on party day. Photos travel directly between devices — nothing goes to the internet.

🏆 Award Voting

Each agent — tap your name and vote for your favourites!

👇 Who is voting?
Voting as:
🎂💥🍰

CAKE FIGHT!

Before the chaos begins — the OFFICIAL rules. Read them. Learn them. Try to follow them.

✅ YOU MAY:
🧽Use your sponge to apply cream — gently. Professor Baker approves of controlled squirting.
🍦Spray cream at torsos and arms only — the legal target zone.
😂Laugh uncontrollably. This is encouraged. In fact it is mandatory.
🤝Negotiate temporary alliances. Betrayals are also acceptable.
🏳️Surrender by waving a sponge above your head. No one will respect this. But try anyway.
❌ YOU MAY NOT:
🚫Aim at faces, eyes or hair. The assistant has already filled in the incident report form. Do not make it worse.
🍰Throw actual cake. That cake took 2 hours to make and deserves better. Eat it. Then fight.
👓Spray cream at anyone wearing glasses. Basic decency. Even in warfare.
🏃Chase anyone who says stop. If someone says stop — it stops. Full paws down.
📱Spray cream near phones, devices, or the assistant. The assistant is NOT a valid target. (They bought the cream. Show some respect.)
🐕Involve any actual dogs. They will eat all the cream and feel no shame about it.
🎒 Equipment Check
🧽 Sponges — 1 per player
🍦 Spray cream — 1 can per 2–3 children
🛁 Garden / easy-clean area strongly advised
👕 Old clothes on — or be prepared to explain this to the washing machine
On the count of 3... 🎂💥 LET THE CHAOS BEGIN!

✅ Pre-Launch Checklist

Pick one child to be the Chief Agent — they read the mission screens aloud, press Next, and keep the team together. Usually the birthday child!

Generate children in Prep 1 first
